raw_consumer_statement:
A fraudulent charge of {$3500.00} was charged to my AM EX on XX/XX/XXXX. I immediately called AM EX to let them know this was fraud, and they cancelled my card, credited the full amount, and opened a fraud investigation. One month later, they sent me an email and a letter stating that the case was closed, that it was not fraud, and that they had credited the amount back to my card. I have tried multiple times to get a hold of someone in the fraud department to speak with them about this decision, but I was repeatedly told that someone would get back to me. One person got back to me with a case number and asked me to call them back. The number they provided was not the fraud department, and I had to go through multiple steps and answer questions each time to reach the fraud department. The person " XXXX '' was never available and only called back once, and told me she would call again in 5 minutes, but she never did. 

On XX/XX/XXXX. I spoke with someone in the fraud department again, and they said that XXXX had a note in the system instructing me to submit any proof I had that could further help them. They suggested email documentation. I gathered my email documentation and sent it to them two hours later. They still have not opened the email as of XXXX XXXX  XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX. I have an XXXX tool that lets me know if they opened it. I called back again on XX/XX/XXXX, and they told me they need an additional 30 days to investigate and that the amount needs to be paid in the meantime to avoid late fees and high interest rates. They will not provide any feedback other than that they need more time. As a result of this, my credit score has also dropped.
